description: Muscle is the contractile body tissue that produces movement and helps hold a body in position; the noun can also identify one muscle. 'puro' (strength) is a capacity for force, while 'kema' (strong) describes that capacity in context. The heart is muscular, but 'korua' specifies the organ when that is what the sentence means.
articulatory_notes: Close the back of the tongue against the soft palate for unvoiced 'k' and release into high-back rounded 'u'. Make a tap or short trill at the alveolar ridge and continue into stressed middle-height back rounded 'o'. Raise the tongue into high-front unrounded 'i' for the separate final syllable.
usage_notes: Context distinguishes muscle tissue in general from one named or located muscle. A preceding body-part noun narrows the location.

semantic_domains.physical: contractile tissue that moves or stabilises a body
